title: podman system migrate crashes
body:
### Issue Description

Running `podman system migrate` crashes:

```
$ podman system migrate
stopped dd4ac350873cfe9d6e5c5673150af49861a512e404ab9a50dc54632596aa9a8f
WARN[0010] StopSignal SIGTERM failed to stop container vigilant_cerf in 10 seconds, resorting to SIGKILL 
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x0 pc=0x1590adb]

goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*storageService).UnmountContainerImage(0x0, {0xc000681940?, 0x1b?}, 0x0)
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/storage.go:235 +0x3b
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).unmount(0xc0003a9540, 0x40?)
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_internal.go:2488 +0x3a
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).cleanupStorage(0xc0003a9540)
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_internal.go:2019 +0x4c5
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).cleanup(0xc0003a9540, {0x20c0c00, 0x2f40760})
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_internal.go:2114 +0x5fb
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).waitForConmonToExitAndSave(0xc0003a9540)
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_internal.go:1517 +0x4c5
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).stop(0xc0003a9540, 0xa)
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_internal.go:1459 +0x5c5
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).StopWithTimeout(0xc0003a9540?, 0xa?)
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_api.go:320 +0xce
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Container).Stop(...)
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_api.go:290
github.com/containers/podman/libpod.(*Runtime).Migrate(0xc000102540, {0x0, 0x0})
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/runtime_migrate_linux.go:76 +0x2e5
github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi.(*ContainerEngine).Migrate(0x0?, {0x0?, 0xcb2006ca48b37857?}, {{0x0?, 0x0?}})
        github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi/system.go:307 +0x25
github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/system.migrate(0x2e3a000?, {0x2f40760?, 0x0?, 0x0?})
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/system/migrate.go:56 +0x45
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).execute(0x2e3a000, {0xc00003ea40, 0x0, 0x0})
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:989 +0xabb
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteC(0x2e22d20)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1117 +0x44f
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).Execute(...)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1041
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteContext(...)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1034
main.Execute()
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/root.go:119 +0x57
main.main()
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/main.go:62 +0x4b2
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I don't know how to reproduce this issue.

```
        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_api.go:320 +0xce

        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/container_api.go:290

        github.com/containers/podman/libpod/runtime_migrate_linux.go:76 +0x2e5
github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi.(*ContainerEngine).Migrate(0x0?, {0x0?, 0xcb2006ca48b37857?}, {{0x0?, 0x0?}})
        github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi/system.go:307 +0x25
github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/system.migrate(0x2e3a000?, {0x2f40760?, 0x0?, 0x0?})
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/system/migrate.go:56 +0x45
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).execute(0x2e3a000, {0xc00003ea40, 0x0, 0x0})
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:989 +0xabb
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteC(0x2e22d20)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1117 +0x44f
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).Execute(...)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1041
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteContext(...)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1034
main.Execute()
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/root.go:119 +0x57
main.main()
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/main.go:62 +0x4b2
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected that `podman system migrate` succeeds.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: ideally you would be reporting this on main branch or 5.7.  are you in a position to do that ?
also, any chance you could capture a core ?
Is this reproducible? I took a quick look, and it seems like this is caused by `runtime.store` being nil, which shouldn't be possible.

@mheon  @baude  

We are experimenting right now a lot with systemd service hardening and cgroup management. I think you are able to reproduce this issue with a systemd service file like this one:

```
[Unit]
Description=Nginx

[Service]
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
ExecStart=podman kube play --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --replace  --service-container=true nginx.kube
User=<your own user>
Group=<your own user>
Delegate=true


[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

And:

```
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx
  labels:
    app: nginx
spec:
  containers:
    - name: server
      image: docker.io/library/nginx:alpine
      ports:
        - name: http
          containerPort: 12345
```

I am very confident it has to do with changing the cgroup manager.

> @shibumi Like I mentioned in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27499 already we do not support such User/Group= setups and most of these systemd hardening options are fundamentally incompatible with podman since you block podman from doing what it has to do.

Hi,

I am aware of this and the intention of this issue here is mainly to report the panic and nil dereference.

I'm running into a bit of a documentation gap and wanted to share a user's perspective. I've found that I often have to search through GitHub issues to learn whether features like X or Y are supported.

To help make this clearer for everyone, what do you think about adding a section to the man page? Having a clear list of what is supported and what's explicitly not would be a huge help and would also lead to less toil in the form of Github issues.

As a separate idea, I've been exploring systemd service hardening. It would be fantastic if the podman team could share some best practices for this. As a stretch goal, perhaps there's a way we could allow users to inject hardening options directly into the systemd podman generator or Quadlets?

If you know about any hardening options that are incompatible with podman itself, can we get these documented somewhere? For example, what are file paths that podman requires to work?

Can you verify if https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27604 fixes the issue for you. I guess the issue is that your systemd unit kills the pause process which is supposed to outlive the unit normally but I guess it didn't got moved because of the cgroup driver argument.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
